revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:どんと祭]][[ru:Донтосай]][[uz:Dontosay]]
[[File:Donto-sai Festival.jpg|250px|thumb|{{ill|Takekoma Inari Shrine|en|Takekoma Inari Shrine|qid=Q521319|lt=Takekoma Inari Shrine|1=Takekoma Inari Shrine}}]] '''Donto-sai''' (どんと祭) is the name for a {{ill|Festival|en|Festival|qid=Q132241|lt=Festival|1=Festival}} primarily held in {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|en|Miyagi Prefecture|qid=Q47896|lt=Miyagi Prefecture|1=Miyagi Prefecture}}.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|和書|title=仙台発「どんと祭」発展の歴史とは 呼び名は新聞記事がきっかけ |url=https://kahoku.news/articles/20220112khn000025.html |website=河北新報オンラインニュース |date=2022-01-12 |accessdate=2022-01-15 |language=ja|qq=}}</ref> It resembles similar festivals in other regions, such as {{ill|Sagichō Fire Festival|en|Sagichō Fire Festival|qid=Q9332740|lt=Sagichō|1=Sagichō Fire Festival}} or "Dondo-yaki."<ref name=":0" /> == Overview == This festival involves burning New Year decorations at {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shrines|1=Shinto shrine}} and praying for health and family safety for the coming year by being warmed by the sacred fire. It is particularly popular at various shrines across Miyagi Prefecture, with the "'''{{ill|Matsutaki Festival|ja|松焚祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsutaki Festival|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'''" (まつたきまつり) at {{ill|Ōsaki Hachimangū|en|Ōsaki Hachimangū|qid=Q874428|lt=Ōsaki Hachiman Shrine|1=Ōsaki Hachimangū}} in {{ill|Sendai|en|Sendai|qid=Q46747|lt=Sendai|1=Sendai}} being the largest of its kind in the prefecture. The exact origins of the festival are unknown, but it had become an established annual event by {{ill|Kaei|en|Kaei|qid=Q1146125|lt=1849|1=Kaei}} 2 (1849).<ref name=":0" /> Initially, the practice of burning New Year decorations was unique to Ōsaki Hachiman Shrine until about 1880.<ref name=":0" /> The name "Donto-sai" is believed to have been popularized by an article in the local newspaper, {{ill|Kahoku Shimpō|en|Kahoku Shimpō|qid=Q639774|lt=Kahoku Shinpō|1=Kahoku Shimpō}}, on January 14, 1906. The article speculated, without basis, that the name was influenced by a similar event in Kyushu, where participants chanted "Dondo." The shrine's connection to {{ill|Usa Jingū|en|Usa Jingū|qid=Q715632|lt=Usa Shrine|1=Usa Jingū}} in Ōita Prefecture further contributed to this theory.<ref name=":0" /> The festival spread to other shrines outside Sendai after the {{ill|Japanese economic miracle|lt=High Economic Growth Era|ja|高度経済成長期|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese economic miracle|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 高度経済成長|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 高度経済成長|1=Japanese economic miracle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q1059474}}.<ref name=":0" /> In many parts of Miyagi Prefecture, the festival is held on the evening of January 14, the day before {{ill|First Full Moon Festival|en|First Full Moon Festival|qid=Q13423452|lt=Koshōgatsu|1=First Full Moon Festival}}. However, other regions hold it on different days, such as January 15 at {{ill|Morioka Hachimangū|en|Morioka Hachimangū|qid=Q11581351|lt=Morioka Hachimangu Shrine|1=Morioka Hachimangū}} in {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|en|Iwate Prefecture|qid=Q48326|lt=Iwate Prefecture|1=Iwate Prefecture}} or as part of the "Uso-kae Festival" at {{ill|Nishine Shrine|ja|西根神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishine Shrine|qq=}} in {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|en|Fukushima Prefecture|qid=Q71707|lt=Fukushima Prefecture|1=Fukushima Prefecture}}. In {{ill|Ishinomaki|en|Ishinomaki|qid=Q270814|lt=Ishinomaki|1=Ishinomaki}}, it is held on January 7, a practice established during the {{ill|New Life Movement (Japan)|ja|新日本建設国民運動|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Life Movement (Japan)|qq=}} in the 1970s.<ref name="sanriku-kahoku20060108">[http://www.sanriku-kahoku.com/news/2006_01/i/060108i-donto.html 炎に祈る無病息災] {{webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160305031334/http://www.sanriku-kahoku.com/news/2006_01/i/060108i-donto.html |date=2016th year 3月5日 |qq=}}（{{ill|Sanriku Kahoku Shinpō|ja|三陸河北新報社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanriku Kahoku Shinpō|qq=}} 2006th year 1月8日）</ref> Participants in the festival sometimes take part in '''Naked Pilgrimages''' (裸参り). While many follow the same attire and methods as those seen at Ōsaki Hachiman Shrine, some regions have unique variations. For instance, participants in the {{ill|Sanuma Donto Festival|ja|佐沼どんと祭裸参り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanuma Donto Festival|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} carry torches instead of lanterns, while those in {{ill|Kakuda, Miyagi|en|Kakuda, Miyagi|qid=Q696425|lt=Kakuda City|1=Kakuda, Miyagi}} use bird-chasing sticks and chant "Yā, hoi hoi hoi."<ref name="TOMEeventwinter">[http://www.city.tome.miyagi.jp/kankou/spot/eventwinter.html 冬のイベント]（登米市）</ref> While traditionally held at shrines, some locations with Buddhist syncretic practices, such as {{ill|Nakayama Tori Taki Fudōson|ja|中山鳥瀧不動尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakayama Tori Taki Fudōson|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, also host Donto-sai events. Similarly, temples like {{ill|Jōgi Nyorai|ja|定義如来|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōgi Nyorai|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 西方寺 (仙台市)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 西方寺 (仙台市)|1=Saihō-ji (Sendai)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11628333}} and {{ill|Sendai Daikannon|ja|仙台大観音|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sendai Daikannon|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大観密寺|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大観密寺|1=Sendai Daikannon|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q1621882}} conduct the festival.<ref>[http://www.melon.or.jp/melon/contents/Section/Water/wg/profiles/profile002.html 中山鳥瀧不動尊]（{{ill|Miyagi Environment Network|ja|みやぎ・環境とくらし・ネットワーク|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyagi Environment Network|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}）</ref> == Matsutaki Festival == [[File:Osaki Hachimangu Shrine on the Dontosai Festival day.jpg|250px|thumb|Matsutaki Festival at Ōsaki Hachiman Shrine]] This festival, held at {{ill|Ōsaki Hachimangū|en|Ōsaki Hachimangū|qid=Q874428|lt=Ōsaki Hachiman Shrine|1=Ōsaki Hachimangū}}, is a longstanding annual event with a history spanning over 300 years.<ref name=":0" /> It was designated a {{ill|Sendai Intangible Cultural Property|ja|仙台市の無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sendai Intangible Cultural Property|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in 2005.<ref>[http://www.city.sendai.jp/kyouiku/bunkazai/bunkazai/81/index.html 文化財せんだい No.81] {{webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20070808184253/http://www.city.sendai.jp/kyouiku/bunkazai/bunkazai/81/index.html |date=2007th year 8月8日 |qq=}}（{{ill|Sendai City Board of Education|ja|仙台市教育委員会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sendai City Board of Education|qq=}}）</ref> == Naked Pilgrimage == {{main|Sendai Naked Pilgrimage|qq=}} Participants in the naked pilgrimage wear a white headband, loincloth, and straw sandals. After purification rituals in ice-cold water, they carry a bell in one hand and a lantern in the other. Participants chant prayers silently while walking to the sacred fire.<ref name=":0" /> == References == {{Reflist|qq=}}
== See Also == * {{ill|Festival of fire|en|Festival of fire|qid=Q3135724|lt=Fire Festivals|1=Fire Festival}} * {{ill|Hadaka Matsuri|en|Hadaka Matsuri|qid=Q2616539|lt=Hadaka Matsuri|1=Hadaka Matsuri}} * {{ill|Sagichō Fire Festival|en|Sagichō Fire Festival|qid=Q9332740|lt=Sagichō|1=Sagichō Fire Festival}} * {{ill|Shinbutsu-shūgō|en|Shinbutsu-shūgō|qid=Q2279088|lt=Shinbutsu-shūgō|1=Shinbutsu-shūgō}} == External Links == * [http://www.okos.co.jp/oosaki/festival/matsutaki/index.html 大崎八幡宮 松焚祭] * [https://web.archive.org/web/20110923171025/http://www.dento.undo.jp/ 仙臺伝統裸参り保存会] * [http://rar.miyagi.nii.ac.jp/metadata/703 仙台市文化財調査報告書 大崎八幡宮の松焚祭と裸祭り]{{リンク切れ|date=2018th year 3月 |bot=InternetArchiveBot |qq=}}（{{ill|Sendai City Board of Education|ja|仙台市教育委員会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sendai City Board of Education|qq=}}, 2006th year 12月）
{{translated page|ja|どんと祭|version=103462422|comment=Imported full ja history}}
[[ja:どんと祭]]
